purpose: Define when future WellFit agents may extend an existing agent/framework and when they must instead create a new-agent proposal for human review before building anything new. This policy prevents duplicate architecture, silent new agent creation, protected-scope drift, and unauthorized runtime authority while allowing safe, scoped improvements to existing report-only governance.

existingAgentExtensionRules.allowedWhen: Adding new fields to an existing agent register when the fields clearly match that register's existing purpose, schema style, safety boundaries, and source-of-truth links.
existingAgentExtensionRules.requiredEvidence: Name the existing register, quote or summarize its purpose, and explain why the added fields are subordinate to that purpose rather than a new role.

existingAgentExtensionRules.allowedWhen: Adding new validation checks to an existing agent validator when the checks validate the existing register/framework and stay report-only unless the existing policy explicitly permits otherwise.
existingAgentExtensionRules.requiredEvidence: Update the existing validator and quality-gate references without creating a new validator family for the same domain.

existingAgentExtensionRules.allowedWhen: Adding new status values only when compatible with the existing policy lifecycle and without weakening review, blocked, report-only, or protected-area semantics.
existingAgentExtensionRules.requiredEvidence: Document compatibility with existing status transitions and human-review gates.

existingAgentExtensionRules.allowedWhen: Adding new backlog, finding, review, or evidence categories for an existing agent domain such as Website Agent Backlog, PR Review, Continuity Sentinel, Task Status Sync, Product Readiness, or Cross-Reference Maintenance.
existingAgentExtensionRules.requiredEvidence: Map each new category to the existing domain and confirm it does not create a separate architecture.

existingAgentExtensionRules.allowedWhen: Adding cross-reference rules for existing mapped files and registers already represented in Work Map, TODO Index, continuity dependencies, or cross-reference maintenance.
existingAgentExtensionRules.requiredEvidence: List connected files/registers and update their pointers only where needed.

existingAgentExtensionRules.allowedWhen: Improving existing report-only checks, guard summaries, output schemas, or diagnostics without enabling auto-merge, auto-repair, deployment, approval, runtime execution, or product mutation.
existingAgentExtensionRules.requiredEvidence: Validator output must continue to print report-only and forbidden-auto-action confirmations.

existingAgentExtensionRules.allowedWhen: Adding required reads, checks, or handoff evidence to existing task queue entries when the task category already exists and remains within the existing agent's domain.
existingAgentExtensionRules.requiredEvidence: Update the existing task candidate or global read/check list instead of creating a new task queue family.

existingAgentExtensionRules.allowedWhen: Updating Product Readiness or Work Map pointers for existing agents to keep source-of-truth relationships current.
existingAgentExtensionRules.requiredEvidence: Keep edits concise, pointer-only, and synchronized with TODO Index and relevant registers.

newAgentProposalRules.requiresProposalWhen: Creating a new agent name, role, persona, autonomous capability, or durable agent responsibility not already covered by an existing WellFit agent/framework.
Creating a new agent register rather than extending an existing register that already governs the domain.
Creating a new agent workflow family, mode, lifecycle, runner, orchestration layer, or governance family.
Creating a new task queue category not covered by existing agents, existing task candidates, or mapped Product Readiness modules.
The proposed capability overlaps with two or more existing agents/frameworks and cannot be cleanly assigned to one existing owner.

The proposed work touches protected or high-risk areas, including token, NFT, wallet, payment, betting, reward authority, mission completion authority, health, child, location, privacy, legal, compliance, camera, biometric, Unity, PR #13, runtime product code, Firebase Functions, Firestore rules, package manifests, or GitHub workflows.
Adding runtime execution authority, product mutation authority, write-mode automation, external service action authority, or autonomous task execution beyond existing report-only checks.
Adding auto-merge, auto-repair, deployment, self-approval, PR approval, PR close, branch deletion, release, or production-facing behavior.
Changing reward, mission authority, health, child, location, privacy, legal, compliance, token, wallet, payment, betting, NFT, marketplace, Unity, or PR #13 scope.

allowedExtensionExamples.example: Website Agent Framework gets Website Agent Backlog
allowedExtensionExamples.reason: The backlog stores findings, blockers, evidence gaps, and safe next tasks for the already-defined website agent domain.

allowedExtensionExamples.example: PR Review Agent gets PR Diff Review integration
allowedExtensionExamples.reason: PR Diff Review adds changed-file evidence to the existing PR review governance chain without approving, merging, repairing, or deploying.

allowedExtensionExamples.example: Continuity Sentinel gets new must-not-forget entries
allowedExtensionExamples.reason: The entries preserve open points and dependencies inside the sentinel's existing continuity purpose.

allowedExtensionExamples.example: Task Status Sync gets stricter work-log validation
allowedExtensionExamples.reason: The stricter validation enforces the existing task-status/work-log policy and does not create a new work tracking system.

allowedExtensionExamples.example: Product Readiness gets updated next-safe-task metadata
allowedExtensionExamples.reason: The metadata refines existing readiness guidance for an existing module.

allowedExtensionExamples.example: Cross-Reference Maintenance gets new file dependency rules
allowedExtensionExamples.reason: The rules extend the existing cross-reference map for mapped files and registers.

requiresProposalExamples.reason: New behavioral-product capability with possible engagement, wellness, and ethics implications; must be proposed and reviewed before build.
Cross-cuts Product Readiness, Adaptive User Insight, Research/Recommendation, compliance, and user-safety governance; requires proposal to avoid overlap.
Could affect mission generation, reward assumptions, completion authority, and user-facing runtime flows.
May overlap Repository Inventory, Product Readiness, Research/Recommendation, Adaptive User Insight, analytics, and roadmap governance.
Would define or curate future agents and therefore needs explicit human approval before becoming an agent.
Would activate merge authority, currently forbidden without explicit human review and policy change.
Would move from report-only repair classification to file mutation authority.
Would modify runtime product code and protected app behavior rather than governance/reporting only.

overlapDetectionRules.rule: Before adding capability, list all existing agents/frameworks/registers that would read, validate, report, decide, or own the same evidence. Count any non-trivial owner as one connected agent.
If exactly one existing agent clearly owns the domain and the change fits that purpose, prefer an extension to that agent/register.
If two or more existing agents have plausible ownership and the change creates new responsibilities, require a new-agent proposal or an explicit human decision assigning the extension to one owner.
Protected/high-risk topics override extension eligibility and require human review even when an existing agent seems to own the area.

If the same purpose already exists in Work Map, TODO Index, Product Readiness, Cross-Reference Maintenance, Continuity Sentinel, PR Review, Website Agents, Research/Recommendation, Adaptive User Insight, Autopilot, Batch Execution Runner, or Repository Inventory, do not create a duplicate system.

connectedAgentImpactRules: Always identify connected agents/frameworks by name before extending or proposing: Autopilot, Batch Execution Runner, PR Review, PR Diff Review, Post-PR Guard, Task Status Sync, Continuity Sentinel, Website Agent Framework, Website Agent Backlog, Product Readiness, Repository Inventory, Cross-Reference Maintenance, Research/Recommendation, Adaptive User Insight, or any newer registered framework.
For each connected agent, state whether the change is read-only/report-only, registry-only, validator-only, task-selection guidance, or proposed new execution authority.
If connected agents disagree or ownership is ambiguous, stop at proposal/reporting and request human review.
Do not let one agent silently grant authority to another; authority changes require explicit human review and updated source-of-truth registers.
connectedRegisterImpactRules: Count connected registers when they are read, validated, referenced in output, or used to decide status/priority/risk.

At minimum consider agent-workflows, agent-task-queue, agent-autopilot, continuity-dependency-map, cross-reference-maintenance, task-status-policy, pr-review-policy, website-agents, website-agent-backlog, product-readiness, repository-inventory, risk-classifier, definition-of-done, and TODO/Work Map files.
Update Work Map and TODO Index pointers whenever a new policy/doc/script becomes a durable governance source.
Preserve historical TODO/status context; stale or duplicate items must be marked and linked, not deleted.
Register impact is not implementation approval; it is evidence for human review and future task selection.
humanReviewRequiredFor: new agent name, role, persona, or durable agent responsibility
new agent register or workflow family
overlap with two or more existing agents/frameworks
runtime execution authority or product mutation authority
auto-merge, auto-repair, deployment, self-approval, PR approval, PR close, branch deletion, or release behavior
reward, mission completion authority, health, child, location, privacy, legal, compliance, token, NFT, wallet, payment, betting, Unity, or PR #13 impact
unclear ownership, unclear risk, or possible duplicate architecture
status transition that weakens review-required, blocked, report-only, or protected-area semantics

requiredSections: Work Map and TODO Index references
